etymology.description: 茅(茅) (máo, "reeds") refers to a type of grass, which might have been abundant or distinctive in the geographical area. 臺(台) (tái, "platform") can refer to a flat area or a place of significance. In this compound, it forms the name of the place 茅台(茅台) (Máotáitáij, "Moutai"), which is famous for its liquor.
etymology.interpretation: 茅(茅) (máo, "reeds") and 臺(台) (tái, "platform") together specify a particular geographical setting associated with the production of high-quality liquor. This compound strictly represents a place name, famously associated with 茅臺酒(茅台酒) (Máotái jiǔ), a type of distilled Chinese liquor. The name suggests a unique locality or community known for its skilled liquor production, akin to terroir in winemaking.

examples.chinese: 茅台(茅台)是一种非常著名的中国白酒(是一種非常著名的中國白酒)。
examples.pinyin: Máotái shì yī zhǒng fēicháng zhùmíng de Zhōngguó báijiǔ.
examples.english: Moutai is a very famous Chinese spirit.

examples.chinese: 在婚宴上(在婚宴上)，他们总会喝茅台(他們總會喝茅台)。
examples.pinyin: Zài hūnyàn shàng, tāmen zǒng huì hē máotái.
examples.english: At weddings, they always drink Moutai.

examples.chinese: 他收藏了许多不同年份的茅台(他收藏了許多不同年份的茅台)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shōucángle xǔduō bùtóng niánfèn de máotái.
examples.english: He has collected many different vintages of Moutai.
